content: We can frame the intrinsic reward gap between ICM (which uses prediction error for curiosity) and RND (which uses random network prediction error) as an active memory pruning signal for state-of-the-art 2024-2025 episodic agent memory: large, consistent prediction gaps between the two curiosity methods mark unexpected, high-value novel states that should be retained, while small overlapping gaps signal familiar low-information states that can be pruned to reduce memory bloat. This creates a self-supervised memory filtering mechanism that requires no external reward labels.
source_topics: curiosity driven reinforcement learning ICM RND compared to curious agent
trigger_topic: curiosity driven reinforcement learning ICM RND compared to curious agent
